verdicts.name: Tosti  Prosecco DOC Extra Dry
verdicts.reason: Prosecco DOC Extra Dry is labeled 'Extra Dry' which per the sparkling dosage ladder (12-17 g/L residual sugar) is SWEETER than Brut, placing it in the Off-Dry category, NOT Dry. The label term 'Extra Dry' is a style designation that indicates sweetness relative to other sparkling categories, not a palate-dry classification.

verdicts.name: Johnnie Walker  Double Black (1 L)
verdicts.reason: Johnnie Walker Double Black is a blended Scotch whisky known for its rich, smoky, and robust character with higher alcohol content (40% ABV) and intense flavoring from peated malts. Full body is the appropriate descriptor for this product.

verdicts.name: Dalwhinnie  15 Years (700 ml)
verdicts.reason: Dalwhinnie 15 is a Highland single malt known for its light, delicate, and floral profile. It is one of the lightest-bodied whiskies in the Diageo Classic Malts range. Light body is the correct descriptor.

verdicts.name: Lagavulin  16 Years (700 ml)
verdicts.reason: Lagavulin 16 is an Islay single malt known for rich, heavy, peaty character with substantial mouthfeel. Full body is the correct classification for this whisky.

verdicts.name: Cragganmore  12 Years (700 ml)
verdicts.reason: Cragganmore 12 Years is a Speyside single malt with moderate alcohol (40% ABV), unpeated character, and typical Speyside profile: floral, fruity, elegant. Medium body is the standard classification for this distillery style—not light (like Glenmorangie) nor full (like Macallan or peated malts). Correct.

verdicts.name: Johnnie Walker  Black Label (1 L)
verdicts.reason: Johnnie Walker Black Label is a blended Scotch whisky with a mature, rich profile (12 years minimum age statement). Medium-Full body is accurate for this category—it has more weight and texture than lighter blends but less intensity than cask-strength or heavily peated single malts. The blend's composition (Talisker, Cardhu, Cragganmore, Oban) supports this assessment.

verdicts.name: Johnnie Walker  Black Label (700 ml)
verdicts.reason: Johnnie Walker Black Label is a blended Scotch whisky with a mature, rich profile (12 years minimum age statement). This is the standard industry assessment for this product.

verdicts.name: Johnnie Walker  Black Ruby (1 L)
verdicts.reason: Johnnie Walker Black Label is a blended Scotch whisky with a balanced, medium body—not full-bodied. Full body typically requires higher proof, longer maturation, or peated/sherried components. Black Label is approachable and lighter than premium expressions like Blue Label or Gold Label Reserve.

verdicts.name: Johnnie Walker  Red Label (1 L)
verdicts.reason: Johnnie Walker Red Label is a blended Scotch whisky with a standard, approachable profile. Medium body is the correct classification—it is lighter than premium expressions (Gold/Black Label) but not as light as grain-forward blends. This is consistent with its composition and market positioning.

verdicts.name: Ron Zacapa  Centenario Sistema Solera 23 Years (750 ml)
verdicts.reason: Ron Zacapa Centenario 23 is a premium aged rum with 23 years maturation in solera system, high ABV (~40%), and rich molasses base. Extended barrel aging and solera process produce full body with viscosity and weight. Full body is correct.

verdicts.name: Ron Zacapa  Centenario XO Rum Solera Gran Reserva Especial (750 ml)
verdicts.reason: Ron Zacapa Centenario XO is a premium aged rum (solera system, 23+ years average age) with high ABV (~40%) and rich, complex flavor profile from extended barrel maturation. Full body is the correct classification for this spirit.

verdicts.name: Johnnie Walker  Gold Label Reserve (750 ml)
verdicts.reason: Johnnie Walker Gold Label Reserve is a blended Scotch whisky with a mature, rich profile (18+ year blend). Medium-Full body is appropriate for its age statement, cask maturation, and blended composition. No domain rule violation.

verdicts.name: Johnnie Walker  Gold Label XR 21 (750 ml)
verdicts.reason: Johnnie Walker Gold Label XR 21 is a premium blended Scotch whisky with 21 years maturation. Medium-Full is the appropriate descriptor for this product's weight and texture.

verdicts.name: Johnnie Walker  Green Label (750 ml)
verdicts.reason: Johnnie Walker Green Label is a blended malt whisky with a balanced, approachable profile. Medium body is the standard classification for this expression—it sits between lighter blends and fuller-bodied single malts, consistent with its composition of malts from Talisker, Cragganmore, Cardhu, and Oban.

verdicts.name: Johnnie Walker  Blue Label (750 ml)
verdicts.reason: Johnnie Walker Blue Label is a premium blended Scotch whisky with a high proportion of aged malts (minimum 25+ years). Its rich, complex profile with deep oak influence, smooth mouthfeel, and substantial weight on the palate justifies a Full body classification.

verdicts.name: Singleton  Of Dufftown 12 Year Old (700 ml)
verdicts.reason: Singleton of Dufftown 12 YO is a Speyside single malt known for smooth, approachable character with moderate weight. Medium body is the standard profile for this distillery house style—not light (like Glenmorangie), not full (like Macallan). Correct.

verdicts.name: Talisker  18 Years (700 ml)
verdicts.reason: Talisker 18 Years is a premium, aged single malt (18 years) from Islay with high alcohol content (~45.8% ABV) and rich, complex profile. Extended maturation and Islay character (peaty, maritime) produce a full-bodied whisky. Full body is correct.

verdicts.name: Talisker  10 Years (700 ml)
verdicts.reason: Talisker is a peated Islay single malt from a distillery known for smoky character. Per domain rules, Talisker is explicitly listed as a peat-producing distillery (smoky even without 'peat' in name). Current value 'none' is incorrect.

verdicts.name: Talisker  10 Years (700 ml)

verdicts.name: Captain Morgan  White Rum (700 ml)

While lighter than dark/aged rums, it has more body than a true light rum (typically unaged, neutral). Medium body is the accurate classification for this commercial spiced/flavored white rum.

verdicts.name: Bulleit  Bourbon (700 ml)
verdicts.reason: Bulleit Bourbon (90 proof, 45% ABV) is a high-rye bourbon with robust grain character and spice notes. Medium-Full body is accurate for this proof level and style—fuller than standard bourbons but not as heavy as cask-strength expressions.

verdicts.name: Bulleit  Rye (700 ml)
verdicts.reason: Bulleit Rye (95% rye, 5% malt) is a high-rye bourbon-style whiskey with robust spice and grain character. At 90 proof (45% ABV) with significant rye content, it delivers a medium-full body—fuller than light ryes but not as heavy as high-proof or heavily-aged expressions. The current value is accurate.

verdicts.name: Tanqueray  No. TEN Distilled Gin (700 ml)
verdicts.reason: Tanqueray No. TEN is a London Dry Gin with a balanced, smooth profile. It uses fresh citrus botanicals (grapefruit, lime) and chamomile, resulting in a crisp yet rounded mouthfeel. verdicts.name: Tanqueray  Gin (750 ml)

verdicts.name: Tanqueray  Flor de Sevilla (700 ml)
verdicts.reason: Tanqueray Flor de Sevilla is a citrus-forward gin (Seville orange infusion) with standard London Dry base spirit. verdicts.name: Don Julio  Blanco (750 ml)
verdicts.reason: Don Julio Blanco is an unaged tequila with typical agave-forward profile and standard distillation. verdicts.name: Don Julio  Ultima Reserva Tequila (750 ml)

verdicts.name: Don Julio  1942 (1.75 L)

verdicts.name: Don Julio  1942 (750 ml)
verdicts.reason: Don Julio 1942 is an extra-aged añejo tequila (aged 2.5+ years) with high agave concentration and oak maturation, producing a rich, viscous, full-bodied profile. Full body is the correct assessment.

verdicts.name: Don Julio  Reposado (750 ml)
verdicts.reason: Don Julio Reposado is aged 2-11 months in oak, developing moderate richness and weight. Medium body is the standard profile for reposado tequilas—fuller than blanco but lighter than añejo. This is correct.

verdicts.name: Gordon's  Dry Gin (700 ml)
verdicts.reason: Gordon's Dry Gin is a London Dry style gin with a light, delicate body typical of the category. 'Dry' is a style name (not palate descriptor per rules), so we judge actual body: gin spirits are inherently light-bodied due to distillation and ABV (~40%). Light is correct.

verdicts.name: Glenmorangie  Grand Vintage 1997 (700 ml)
